# Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) — Investment Memo

**Business context:** Live Nation's revenue has scaled dramatically post-pandemic, from $6.3B in FY2021 [F1] to $25.2B in FY2025 [F5], though growth has decelerated sharply — from 166.1% YoY in 2022 [M5] to just 8.8% in 2025 [M17].

**Profitability:** The company swung from a net loss of $650.9M in 2021 [F6] to net income of $266.4M in 2022 [F7], $556.9M in 2023 [F8], and a peak of $896.3M in 2024 [F9], before falling to $496.0M in 2025 [F10] — a 44.7% YoY decline [M18]. Net margins have remained thin throughout, moving from -10.4% in 2021 [M1] to 3.9% in 2024 [M11] and back down to 2.0% in 2025 [M15], reflecting the low-margin nature of the concert promotion/ticketing model.

**Balance sheet:** Stockholders' equity was deeply negative in 2021 (-$582.7M) [F21] and 2022 (-$367.6M) [F22], turned less negative at -$52.3M in 2023 [F23], and went positive at $173.3M in 2024 [F24] and $271.0M in 2025 [F25]. Total assets grew from $14.4B in 2021 [F16] to $22.9B in 2025 [F20]. Because equity was near zero or negative for most of this period, ROE figures are extreme and not meaningful in the traditional sense — e.g., 517.3% in 2024 [M12] and 183.0% in 2025 [M16].

**Cash generation:** Operating cash flow has been consistently strong and less volatile than net income, ranging from $1.36B (2023) [F13] to $1.84B (2022) [F12], with $1.40B
judgePairs.ours:
# LYV — Investment Memo

**Overview**

Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) has shown a dramatic revenue expansion over the past several years, driven by the live events and ticketing business. Revenue grew from $6.3B in FY2021 to $25.2B in FY2025, with particularly sharp acceleration in FY2022 ($16.7B) [F2] after the pandemic-induced trough in FY2021 ($6.3B) [F1]. Revenue growth has since moderated, to 36.2% in FY2023 [M9] and 1.9% in FY2024 [M13], with a modest pickup to 8.8% in FY2025 [M17].

**Profitability**

Net income has tracked a volatile path alongside revenue. LYV posted a loss of $650.9M in FY2021 [F6], then turned positive with $266.4M in FY2022 [F7], $556.9M in FY2023 [F8], $896.3M in FY2024 [F9], and $496.0M in FY2025 [F10]. Net margin was negative in FY2021 (-10.38%) [M1], then positive but thin in FY2022 (1.6%) [M3], FY2023 (2.5%) [M7], FY2024 (3.9%) [M11], and FY2025 (1.97%) [M15].

Return on equity has been highly volatile. In FY2021, LYV generated a return of 111.7% [M2] on negative equity (-$582.7M) [F21], a result of the accounting treatment of the company's capital structure during the merger period. This distorted the metric, and ROE was negative in FY2022 (-72.5%) [M4] and FY2023 (-1065%) [M8], before improving to 517.3% [M12] in FY2024 and 183.0% [M16] in FY2025, as equity turned positive and grew [F24, F25].

**Cash Flow and Balance Sheet**

Operating cash

CME Group — Investment Memo

**Overview**
CME Group has grown revenue steadily from $4.7B in FY2021 to $6.5B in FY2025 [F1][F5], with the most recent year showing revenue growth of 6.4% [M22]. Net income has grown even faster, from $2.6B [F6] to $4.1B [F10], including 15.5% growth in FY2025 [M23].

**Profitability**
CME's net margin has expanded from 56.2% in 2021 [M1] to 62.5% in 2025 [M19], reflecting strong operating leverage typical of an exchange business model. Return on equity has also improved steadily, from 9.6% in 2021 [M3] to 14.2% in 2025 [M21], with 2024 ROE at 13.3% [M16].

**Cash Generation**
Operating cash flow has grown from $2.4B in 2021 [F11] to $4.3B in 2025 [F15], outpacing revenue growth and suggesting healthy underlying cash conversion.

**Balance Sheet**
Total assets stood at $198.4B in 2025 [F20], up sharply from $137.4B in 2024 [F19], while liabilities rose to $169.7B [F25] from $111.0B [F24]. This drove liabilities-to-assets back up to 85.5% [M20] after declining to 79.4%-80.7% in 2023-2024 [M10][M15]. Stockholders' equity has been comparatively stable, ending 2025 at $28.7B [F30], close to the $27.4B level in 2021 [F26]. Diluted weighted-average shares outstanding have been essentially flat, from 358.9M in 2021 [F31] to 360.3M in 2025 [F35], indicating minimal dilution.

# CME Group — Investment Memo

**Overview**

CME Group, the largest derivatives exchange operator, has shown a generally positive trend in revenue and net income over the past five fiscal years, though with some volatility. Revenue grew from $4.7B in FY2021 [F1] to $6.5B in FY2025 [F5], with a peak of $5.6B in FY2023 [F3] before a smaller increase to $6.1B in FY2024 [F4]. Net income also trended upward, from $2.6B in FY2021 [F6] to $4.1B in FY2025 [F10], with a notable acceleration in FY2023 [F8] and FY2025 [F10].

**Profitability**

Net margins have fluctuated around 55-58% over the period, with a low of 53.6% in FY2022 [M4] and a high of 57.8% in FY2023 [M9], before softening slightly to 57.5% in FY2024 [M14] and improving again to 62.5% in FY2025 [M19]. Return on equity (ROE) has been modest, ranging from 9.6% in FY2021 [M3] to 14.2% in FY2025 [M21], with a trough of 10.0% in FY2022 [M6].

**Balance Sheet**

Total assets declined from $196.8B in FY2021 [F16] to $129.7B in FY2023 [F18], then recovered to $137.4B in FY2024 [F19] and rose sharply to $198.4B in FY2025 [F20]. Total liabilities followed a similar pattern, falling from $169.4B in FY2021 [F21] to $103.0B in FY2023 [F23], then rising to $111.0B in FY2024 [F24] and $169.7B in FY2025 [F25]. The liabilities-to-assets ratio was 86.1% in FY2021 [M2], declined to 79.4% in FY2023 [M10], and rose again to 85.5% in FY2025 [M2

# Prudential Financial (PRU) — Investment Memo

**Business trajectory.** PRU's top line has been volatile: revenue fell from $71.2B in FY2021 [F1] to $56.9B in FY2022 [F2], a 20.2% decline [M7], then to $54.0B in FY2023 [F3] (down 5.1% [M12]), before rebounding 30.4% [M17] to $70.4B in FY2024 [F4], then falling back 13.7% [M22] to $60.8B in FY2025 [F5]. This swings likely reflect insurance/annuity liability marks and market-sensitive items rather than a steady operating trend.

**Profitability.** Net income has been erratic: a loss of $1.6B in FY2022 [F7] (net margin -2.9% [M4]) followed by recovery to $2.5B in FY2023 [F8] (margin 4.6% [M9]), $2.7B in FY2024 [F9] (margin 3.9% [M14]), and $3.6B in FY2025 [F10] (margin 5.9% [M19], up 31.1% y/y [M23]). This is well below the 12.5% margin posted in FY2021 [M1]. Return on equity has similarly recovered from -5.4% in FY2022 [M6] to 11.0% in FY2025 [M21], still short of the 14.3% seen in FY2021 [M3].

**Cash generation.** Operating cash flow has stayed positive throughout: $9.8B (FY2021) [F11], $5.2B (FY2022) [F12], $6.5B (FY2023) [F13], $8.5B (FY2024) [F14], and $6.3B (FY2025) [F15] — supporting the view that reported net income volatility is partly accounting/mark-driven rather than a cash story.

**Balance sheet.** Total assets stand at $773.7B [F20] against liabilities of $738.2B [F25], a liabilities-to-assets ratio of 95.4% [M20]

# Adobe (ADBE) — Investment Memo

**Revenue growth remains consistent but decelerating from earlier highs.** Adobe grew revenue from $15.8B in FY2021 [F1] to $23.8B in FY2025 [F5], a steady climb through $17.6B (FY2022) [F2], $19.4B (FY2023) [F3], and $21.5B (FY2024) [F4]. YoY growth has held in a narrow band: 11.5% (2022) [M7], 10.2% (2023) [M12], 10.8% (2024) [M17], and 10.5% (2025) [M22] — durable, but not accelerating.

**Profitability improved sharply in FY2025.** Net income rose from $4.8B (FY2021) [F6] to $7.1B (FY2025) [F10], with FY2025 net income growth of 28.2% [M23] far outpacing revenue growth, lifting net margin to 30.0% [M19] — the highest in the five-year window, alongside 30.6% in FY2021 [M1]. Margins had dipped to 25.9% in FY2024 [M14] before this rebound.

**Cash generation is strong and improving.** Operating cash flow grew from $7.2B (FY2021) [F11] to $10.0B (FY2025) [F15], with a notable jump from $8.1B (FY2024) [F14] to $10.0B (FY2025) [F15].

**Capital structure has shifted toward more leverage.** Liabilities-to-assets rose from 45.7% (FY2021) [M2] to 60.6% (FY2025) [M20], while stockholders' equity fell from $16.5B (FY2023) [F28] to $11.6B (FY2025) [F30] — likely reflecting buybacks, evidenced by diluted share count declining from 481.0M (FY2021) [F31] to 427.0M (FY2025) [F35]. # Adobe (ADBE) — Investment Memo

**Overview**

Adobe has shown consistent revenue growth across fiscal years 2021 through 2025, with revenue increasing from $15.8B [F1] to $23.8B [F5]. Net income also grew over this period, from $4.8B [F6] to $7.1B [F10], though the path was not linear — net income declined in fiscal 2022 [F7] before recovering in fiscal 2023 [F8]. Operating cash flow has been strong and generally trending upward, from $7.2B [F11] to $10.0B [F15].

**Profitability**

Net margins have trended downward over the period, from 30.6% [M1] in fiscal 2021 to 30.0% [M19] in fiscal 2025, with a dip to 25.9% [M14] in fiscal 2024. Return on equity was 32.6% [M3] in fiscal 2021, rose to 33.9% [M6] in fiscal 2022, and then declined to 32.9% [M11] in fiscal 2023 before rebounding strongly to 61.3% [M21] in fiscal 2025. This jump in ROE in fiscal 2025 was driven in part by a contraction in equity from $16.5B [F28] to $11.6B [F30], reflecting share buybacks or other balance sheet actions rather than earnings growth alone.

**Balance Sheet**

Total assets grew from $27.2B [F16] to $29.5B [F20], while total liabilities rose from $12.4B [F21] to $17.9B [F25]. # ARES — Investment Memo

**Revenue & growth.** ARES revenue has been volatile: $4.2B in FY2021 [F1], falling to $3.1B in FY2022 (-27.5% [M7]), then recovering to $3.6B in FY2023 (+18.9% [M12]), $3.9B in FY2024 (+7.0% [M17]), and $5.6B in FY2025 (+44.2% [M22]). The FY2025 acceleration is the standout data point in the series.

**Profitability.** Net income has swung alongside revenue: $408.8M in FY2021 [F6], dropping to $167.5M in FY2022 (-59.0% [M8]), then $474.3M in FY2023 (+183.1% [M13]), $463.7M in FY2024 (-2.2% [M18]), and $527.4M in FY2025 (+13.7% [M23]). Net margin has ranged from a low of 5.5% in FY2022 [M4] to a high of 25.1%-equivalent ROE year (13.1% net margin in FY2023 [M9]), settling at 9.4% in FY2025 [M19].

**Returns on capital.** Return on equity was strong in FY2021 at 22.4% [M3] and FY2023 at 25.1% [M11], but has since moderated to 13.1% in FY2024 [M16] and 12.3% in FY2025 [M21], despite equity more than doubling from $1.6B in FY2022 [F27] to $4.3B in FY2025 [F30].

**Cash flow.** Operating cash flow tells a notable turnaround story: deeply negative at -$2.6B in FY2021 [F11], improving to -$734.1M in FY2022 [F12] and -$233.3M in FY2023 [F13], before turning strongly positive at $2.8B in FY2024 [F14] and $3.3B in FY2025 [F15]. This shift from cash-burning to cash-generative is arguably the most important inflection in the dataset.

# Ares Management (ARES) — Investment Memo

**Overview**

ARES reported revenue of $4.2B for fiscal year 2021 [F1], which declined to $3.1B in 2022 [F2] before recovering to $3.6B in 2023 [F3] and $3.9B in 2024 [F4]. Revenue surged to $5.6B in 2025 [F5], a year-over-year increase of 44.2% [M22]. Tractor Supply (TSCO) has shown a generally stable trajectory over the five-year period covered by the evidence.
